summary:
PET/CT does not provide significant added benefit over multidetector CT (MDCT) for preoperative staging of colorectal cancer with hepatic metastasis due to its inferior detection of small lesions and higher rate of false positives related to inflammation. Standard CE-CT remains more effective, accurate, accessible, and cost-efficient as first-line imaging for this purpose.

The summary above meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focused strictly on clinically pertinent facts regarding diagnostic performance and utility compared to standard practice.
